channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Haaland scores twice as Norway defeat Brazil 2-1 to qualify for the World Cup quarter-finals
item.description: Norway secured one of the biggest results in their football history after defeating Brazil 2-1 to book a place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. Manchester City striker Erling Haaland was the hero...

item.title: Victorian Women's Fashion and Society in the Mid-19th Century: A Look at 1850s Portraits
item.description: The mid-19th century, during the height of the Victorian era, was marked by elaborate and highly structured women’s fashion. Studio portraits from this period capture the defining styles of the era, s...

item.title: Brake failure lorry crash in Hai District leaves two dead and 12 injured
item.description: Two people lost their lives and 12 others were injured after a serious road accident involving a lorry carrying gravel and a minibus at the Kikafu Bridge area in Mnadani Ward, Hai District, Kilimanjar...

item.title: Vice Ganda Encourages UP CMC Graduates to Advocate for Truth and Justice
item.description: Vice Ganda, a prominent Filipino comedian and actor, recently addressed recent graduates of the University of the Philippines College of Media and Communication (UP CMC), urging them to harness their ...

item.title: Government Advances SOE Reforms to Enhance Governance and Accountability
item.description: The South African government is advancing comprehensive reforms to modernize and strengthen state-owned enterprises (SOEs), which were implicated in corruption during the state capture era under forme...

item.title: Isaac del Toro claims Tour de France Stage 2 victory in Barcelona as Vingegaard retains overall lead
item.description: Mexican cyclist Isaac del Toro secured an impressive victory in Stage 2 of the 2026 Tour de France, winning the uphill finish at Montjuïc in Barcelona ahead of his UAE Team Emirates teammate and defen...

item.title: Vice Ganda and Vic Sotto team up for the upcoming film 'The Greatest Showdown'
item.description: Veteran comedian and television host Vic Sotto and multimedia star Vice Ganda are set to headline the upcoming film 'The Greatest Showdown', bringing together two of the biggest names in Philippine en...

item.title: Senator Rodante Marcoleta's 2025 Election Campaign Spending Breakdown
item.description: Senator Rodante Marcoleta's 2025 election campaign spending of P112.9 million has sparked scrutiny over transparency and funding sources. His Statement of Contributions and Expenditures (SOCE) lists n...

item.title: Punjab Police Investigates Alleged Kidnapping of Foreign Nationals
item.description: The article details the investigation into the alleged kidnapping and sexual assault of two foreign women in Lahore. Punjab Police confirmed that the suspects, including Muhammad Raza Dar (grandson of...

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway's $6.8 billion acquisition of Taylor Morrison and its impact on the US housing market
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway's all-cash acquisition of home builder Taylor Morrison for $6.8 billion is expected to stimulate investment in the US housing market, according to analysts. The deal, finalized in M...

item.title: Gombe Police Arrest Eight Suspects Over Robbery and Mobile Phone-Linked Bank Fraud
item.description: The Gombe State Police Command has arrested eight suspects believed to be members of a criminal syndicate involved in armed robbery and bank fraud across different communities in the state. According ...

item.title: FIFA Suspends Balogun's World Cup Ban After Controversial Red Card
item.description: FIFA's disciplinary committee has suspended Folarin Balogun's one-game World Cup ban, allowing the U.S. striker to participate in the final 16 match against Belgium. Balogun was initially shown a stra...

item.title: Hubble observations of LH 95 provide new insights into the long-term growth of young stars
item.description: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has produced a detailed image of LH 95, a large star-forming region located in the Large Magellanic Cloud, a dwarf galaxy that orbits the Milky Way. The image reveals bri...

item.title: Belgium criticises FIFA after Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension is suspended ahead of last-16 match
item.description: Belgium's football federation has strongly criticised FIFA after the governing body suspended the one-match ban imposed on United States striker Folarin Balogun, making him eligible to play in the Wor...

item.title: Victorian Health Providers Face Scrutiny Over Sexual Misconduct Allegations
item.description: A new report reveals over 230 Victorian health providers have been investigated for alleged sexual misconduct over six years, with massage therapists accounting for the highest number of claims. The H...

item.title: Shooting at Coney Island Cookout Leaves Eight Injured, Four Children Among Victims
item.description: A shooting at a Fourth of July cookout near New York’s Coney Island beach left eight people wounded, including four children, according to police. The incident occurred Saturday night in the courtyard...

item.title: Norway Advances to World Cup Quarterfinals After Defeating Brazil
item.description: In a stunning upset, Norway secured a historic victory over Brazil in their World Cup last-16 clash, sending the Scandinavian nation to their first-ever World Cup quarterfinal. Erling Haaland, the Nor...

item.title: Alzheimer's Society Calls for Dementia to Receive the Same Priority as Cancer in the UK Healthcare System
item.description: The chief executive of the Alzheimer's Society, Michelle Dyson, has criticized the UK's National Health Service (NHS) and government for failing to provide adequate care and support for people living ...

item.title: Mumbai Faces Severe Weather Crisis: Schools Closed, Structural Collapses Reported
item.description: Mumbai schools and colleges remain closed on Monday due to an 'orange alert' issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD), warning of heavy rainfall and gusty winds. The Brihanmumbai Municipal ...

item.title: Filipino fishers report continued Chinese Coast Guard presence and harassment in the West Philippine Sea nearly 10 years after Hague ruling
item.description: Nearly 10 years after the July 12, 2016 arbitral ruling that invalidated China's sweeping claims in the South China Sea, Filipino fisherfolk continue to report the regular presence of Chinese Coast Gu...
